ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature LawChapter 4:Chapter 4:
Creature TalentsCreature Talents
32
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
Besides the normal talents for characters, Creature
Law has a gigantic and diverse selection of other
talents and abilities. These range from having some
special armoring, to being able to breathe fire, to hav-
ing flippers and fins to propel the creature through the
water. Such talents set a creature apart from a charac-
ter. Although the GM may allow a character to possess
a talent from the creature list, it is recommended that
this not be allowed unless, of course, the character
belongs to a race of creatures which has this ability.
These talents are usually above the power and scope
of the talents allowed to the player characters and may
not be appropriate for normal settings.
That said, it is possible to create a race from every crea-
ture, allowing the GM to shape any NPC in the way
he wants, making it possible to develop pets, monster
NPCs, and even player-characters with a monster race.
The GM is advised to take extreme caution with this
approach in terms of game balance. Also, the general
populace does not take well to monstrous characters,
making it necessary to avoid attention and population
centers.
Each talent adds not only some ability or power, but of
course a corresponding bio-mechanical addition. Fire
breath may require special glands, gills would show
on the outside of the neck or face, and pincer attacks
would require some modification to the creature’s
hands or feet. The GM should keep control of these
modifications and base the reactions of the populace
accordingly. Likewise, creatures with talents do nor-
mally look a certain way and GMs should make an
effort to describe a creature’s talents and their work-
ings to enhance his game and the players’ experience.
Balorus the Mitt has inherited a single pincer and
some armored plates on his torso and arms from a
distant ancestor who apparently was a lobster. He
hides his pincer hand in a large oven mitt and he al-
ways wears long sleeved garments, but when he must
punish one of his underlings, he likes to confront
them shirtless and slowly uncover his pincer before
he shears off their finger.
Also, sometimes creatures have extraordinary talents
that fall outside the norm in terms of range, power and
effect. Such talents are followed by modifier talents
that always start with Increase X or Decrease Y. Sim-
ply add the value of the modifier talent to the previous
talent. Sometimes more than one applies.
Teridas, a Dragonian, can spit fire in a long stream.
His special glands and his Dragonian lungs increase
the range of this stream. Normally the Elemental bolt
V talent has a range of 5'/ lvl and the Increase range
II talent adds another 5'/ lvl, for a total of 10'/ lvl or
<2'>/ lvl range increments.
4.1. talent categoRies
Creature talents and flaws are categorized using the
following categories: Attack, Defense, Miscellaneous,
Movement, Physical, and Senses. These are arbitrary
categories and do not have the same meaning as those
in the RMCL. Where the RMCL may be used to re-
strict and bar access to certain talents to certain char-
acters, the Creature Law categories are used for easy
reference when looking up a creature and playing an
encounter.
Talents and flaws found in RMCL are not repeated
here. See RMCL for details of these talents and flaws.
ATTACK
These talents add attacks, or damage to attacks, or
attack-like abilities. This category enhances the chal-
lenge of a creature by enhancing its ability to deal
damage. Also, the offensive related flaws are found
here for ease of use.
Accurate Attacks ........................................ 20 / Tier
The creature’s attacks with a particular Combat Train-
ing specialization are precise and deadly. If a critical is
scored, it gains a bonus of +5/Tier on the critical roll.
Accurate Attacks, Slaying ......................... 10 / Tier
The creature is particularly precise or has special pow-
```
er against a certain kind of foe (e.g. all Orcs, or all
```
```
Huge foes). Its attacks against such foes are treated as
```
Slaying, gaining a bonus of +5/Tier on the critical roll.
Additional Critical ..................................... 10 / Tier
When the creature’s attacks with a particular Combat
Training specialization score a critical, an additional
critical is also inflicted of a specified type. For exam-
ple, flaming claws might inflict an additional Heat
critical, or serrated claws might inflict an additional
Slashing critical. The additional critical uses the same
critical roll as the original critical. The maximum se-
```
verity of the additional critical (after any adjustments
```
```
for size or other factors) is limited by the Tier: severity
```
A for Tier I, B for Tier II, etc.
4. Creature Talents4. Creature Talents
33
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
Attack Sequence ......................................... 10 / Tier
The creature has multiple attacks designed to be
used together. When the first attack scores a critical,
the creature follows it with the second attack. For
```
example, a Ram might lead to a Trample (note that
```
a Trample attack can only occur if the attacker is +2
sizes greater than the target, or +1 size larger if the
```
target has been knocked prone by the attacker), or a
```
Grapple to a Bite. The follow-up attack is treated as an
```
Instantaneous Action (0 AP) with no penalty for using
```
```
fewer AP. With one Tier (Shown as “>” in table) of
```
this talent, the follow-up attack occurs the following
```
round. With two Tiers (Shown as “>>” in table), the
```
follow-up attack occurs immediately.
Aura, Critical........................................ 15 + 5 / Tier
Anyone within a 5' radius of the creature suffers a
```
critical of the specified type (e.g. Heat). The severity
```
of the critical is A for Tier I, B for Tier II, etc. The
severity is not adjusted for the target or attacker size.
Aura, Fear............................................. 10 + 5 / Tier
The creature is terrifying to behold. Anyone seeing
the creature within a radius of 1' per Tier per level of
the creature must make a Fear RR. Mild failure 1-25:
Balk, Moderate failure 26-50: Retreat, Severe failure
```
51-100: Routed, Extreme failure 100+: Broken (see
```
```
RMCL table 14-2 for details).
```
Aura, Hindering ....................................................30
An overwhelming aura of fear, nausea, or depression
surrounds the creature. Anyone entering a 30' radius
```
of the creature must make a RR against Fear (for fear
```
```
effects), Physical (for nausea effects), or Mentalism
```
```
(for depression effects), or suffer a -20 penalty on all
```
actions until they have been outside the radius for at

ther their own melee range or the creature’s, whichev-

```
imum of +3 sizes) based on the Tier of this talent. At
```

ing creatures of Size Diminutive or lower.

DEFENSE
In this category are talents that increase the challenge
of a creature by making it more resilient against at-
tacks, by adding AT, proofs, immunities, and special
abilities such as auras of elemental energy and others.
Also found here are the susceptibilities and other de-
fense flaws.
Acid Coating ................................................. 5 / Tier
The acidic coating of the creature will damage or de-
stroy weapons striking it. Tier I causes harm to soft
materials including flesh, Tier II harms other organ-
ic materials such as wood, Tier III harms ordinary
metals, Tier IV harms stone, and Tier V harms even
precious metals. Affected items must make a RR at -5/
```
Tier (magic weapons add their bonus) or be destroyed.
```
```
If the weapon is part of the attacker (e.g., a martial
```
```
art attack), the attacker suffers an A critical plus one
```
severity per additional Tier past the first.
Agitated ................................................... 15 / 20 / 50
This creature is spurred to unusual might by a partic-
ular circumstance, such as being formally challenged,
in a competition, or receiving a serious insult. When
this occurs, its Move increases by 50% and it gains the
Proof, Weapon talent of the same tier.
Armor .................................................5 / AT above 1
This Talent covers any and all Natural Armor Types
above the base AT of 1. The variety of natural armors
that creatures can have are very extensive, with multi-
ple specific types within any particular AT value.
Aura, Purification .................................................25
Poisons and diseases that come within a 10' radius are
rendered ineffectual and purified. Magical poisons and
Diseases get a Channeling RR.
Confusion Defense.................................................15
The creature may spend a full round to generate a con-
fusion effect which will prevent foes from attacking
for 1 round per 5 failure unless they make a Mentalism
RR. For example, the foe may see themselves or an
ally in place of the creature.
Critical Reduction ...................................... 10 / Tier
The creature has few weak points or a resilient form.
The severity of any critical dealt to the creature is
reduced by one per Tier. Criticals reduced below A
severity have no effect on the creature.
Dense ........................................................... 15 / Tier
A tough, dense body makes the creature more resilient
against attacks than would be expected. All attacks
against the creature are treated as one size smaller per
Tier. Attacks by the creature are not affected.
Disease Transmission ............................................15
The creature is a carrier of disease, and anyone mak-
ing physical contact with the creature, its personal
possessions, or its blood or other fluids must make a
Physical RR against its level or be infected.

```
cals of one element (earth, fire, ice, light, water, and/
```

```
or wind). Attacks of this elemental type are treated as
```

```
sh(K),” so a creature with immunity to Puncture will
```

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
MOVEMENT
Movement talents assume that most creatures have at
least one type of movement. The more modes of loco-
motion, the more talents they have. However, when a
certain type of movement is not listed, such as swim-
ming type movement, or climbing type movement,
it will be treated as the Awkward Movement flaw.
```
Normally this is handled as tier IV (75% reduction)
```
for most types, derived from the primary, first listed,
movement. Deviations from this will be listed in most
cases.
Most movement talents specify the base movement
```
rate (BMR). This base must be modified by half the
```
creature’s Quickness bonus, their stride, and any other
talents which modify their movement rate in order to
determine the actual BMR of the creature.
Awkward Movement ................................... -5 / Tier
One of the creature’s movement modes, such as walk-
ing, swimming, or flying, is slow and clumsy. Attacks
that depend on movement, such as Trample or Ram,
suffer twice the listed maneuver penalty. In addition,
DB is halved.
Burrowing, Acid ....................................................30
The creature secretes acid which permits it to create
tunnels through soil and even rock. It has a BMR of 8'/
```
round with a maximum pace of Walk (1x). The Move-
```
ment rate is reduced to 25% in stone.
Burrowing, Earth ..................................................20
The creature tunnels through earth, sand, and other
soft materials, but not stone. It has a BMR of 10'/
```
round with a maximum pace of Walk (1x).
```
Burrowing, Stone ..................................................40
The creature is able to burrow through earth, sand,
stone and other materials of similar hardness, leaving
a tunnel of its own circumference. Loose materials
will not support a tunnel and will cave in, but packed
earth and rock may hold their shape. It has a BMR
```
of 12'/round with a maximum pace of Walk (1x). The
```
movement rate is reduced to 25% in stone.
Climbing, Adhesion ...............................................15
The creature clings to surfaces with a special remov-
able glue or microscopic hooks, allowing it to cling
even to smooth surfaces, although it will have trouble
```
with especially slick surfaces (e.g., covered in oil). It
```
```
climbs at a BMR of 26'/round at a Walk (1x) pace. If
```
a maneuver roll is required, it gains a bonus of +10.

```
a BMR of 16'/round at up to a Jog (2x) pace. On nat-
```
ural rock surfaces, a climbing roll is needed at +10.
Climbing, Cling .......................................................5
Using sticky pads or suction, the creature holds to
most surfaces although it cannot maintain a fast pace.
```
It has a climbing BMR of 10'/round at a Walk (1x)
```
pace.
Climbing, Effortless ..............................................20
Whether due to remarkable ability to cling to a surface

Climbing, Grip ......................................................10


or constructions with lots of handholds.
Climbing, Snake ....................................................15

Magic Flight........................................ 10 + 10 / Tier

```
V -25 10% -25 Walk (1x)
```

damaging work to dig it up and transport it.
Shard Speed ...........................................................80
The creature is endowed with blinding speed, but it is
so fast it must slow down when trying to turn, and stop
moving completely to make an attack. It has a BMR of
```
400'/round and a maximum pace of Dash (5x).
```
Slow Ground Movement............................. -3 / Tier
The creature’s movement on the ground is extremely
slow. It may slither, ooze, or pull itself along with its
roots. Tier I: BMR 20'/round with a maximum pace of
```
Run (3x). Tier II: BMR 15'/round, max pace Jog (2x).
```
```
Tier III: BMR 10'/round, maximum pace Jog (2x).
```
```
Tier IV: BMR 5'/round, maximum pace Walk (1x).
```
Swimming ..................................................... 5 / Tier
The creature is to some extent adapted to movement
in the water, ranging from a basic ability to float and
```
paddle (Tier I) to webbed feet (Tier II) or a stream-
```
```
lined form (Tier IV) or even an unnatural ability to
```
```
move without resistance through the water (Tier V).
```
The creature’s swimming BMR, maneuver penalty/
bonus, and maximum pace are shown in the table.
Walking, Multipedal .............................................15
The creature walks on more than four limbs, granting
it additional Speed and stability. It has a BMR of 40'/
```
round with a maximum pace of Sprint (4x) and a +20
```
maneuver bonus.
Walking, Quadrupedal ...........................................5
The creature uses four limbs for locomotion. It has a
good speed, good stability, and good carrying capaci-
ty. It has a BMR of 30'/round with a maximum pace of
```
Dash (5x) and a +10 maneuver bonus.
```
Wings, Flying ...................................... 10 + 10 / Tier
The creature is capable of winged flight. Paces above
```
Run (at Tier III+) may only be achieved while diving
```
```
(Sprint) or power diving (Dash). See RMCL section
```
3.17 for more details, including about gliding.
Wings, Gliding .......................................................10
Using its wings, the creature may glide although it is
not capable of true flight. Using thermals and other
tricks it may be able to prolong its time aloft, but in
the absence of updrafts it will gradually lose altitude.
Gliders have a BMR of 50'/round but can only achieve
```
a Walk (1x) pace in level flight or up to Run (3x) when
```
diving.
PHYSICAL
This category encompasses talents linked with shape
and body. A creature’s form usually reflects its pur-
pose, power and other factors. Physical talents add
special, visible features on a creature, or within the
creature, but they are not offensive or defensive fea-
tures, barring a few exceptions.
Additional Head ................................. 15 + 10 / Tier
The creature possesses one extra head per tier, nor-
mally of the same type and size as the base creature,
although it is also possible for the heads to each be
their own type with their own attacks and senses. Each
head has its own consciousness, but only the first main
head is capable of controlling the body. The additional
heads may execute their own attacks autonomously
```
and keep vigil, talk (if capable) and take their rest
```
separately.
Air and Water Breathing ......................................20
The creature can breathe water as easily as air, allow-
```
ing it to survive easily both in and out of water. (For
```
creatures that breathe water but not air, use the Gills
```
talent instead.)
```
Amphibious.......................................................... -10
Although capable of surviving out of water, the crea-
ture requires immersion in water at regular intervals. It
may stay dry for up to one day before returning to the
water. After this time, it suffers a penalty of -1 per hour
it has spent out of the water. This penalty may only be
removed by immersion in water.
Aura, Elemental Explosion ..................................30
The raw elemental material of the creature continu-
ously blasts in all directions, subjecting everyone
within a 1' per level radius with a +10 Ball attack of
```
the appropriate element every round (use a Small Fire
```
Ball with Impact criticals for Earth and Wind, Me-
dium Cold Ball with Cold criticals for Ice and with
Impact criticals for Water, and a Tiny Lightning Ball
```
for Light).
```

Creature Law
Autonomous Attacks .............................................35
The creature’s limbs move independently. Each may
be controlled by a different head, mind, or spirit. The
creature may make as many attacks as it has limbs as a
single attack action, without any penalty for multiple
attacks.
Body of Smoke .......................................................15
With concentration, the creature may transform to
smoke, able to pass through cracks and small open-
ings. It is immune to any damage in this state but can-
not attack. It may remain in smoke form for up to 10
minutes per hour.
Camouflage, Chameleon .......................................40
The skin of the creature may change to match the
background, making it easy for the creature to remain
concealed. It gains a +50 bonus to Stalking and +200
when motionless.
Camouflage, Pattern .............................................10
The creature’s hide is well suited to concealment in
```
one particular type of terrain (e.g., forest, desert).
```
While in this terrain, it gains a +25 bonus to Stalking.
Deformed Feature ............................................... -10
A hideously deformed feature causes others of its own
kind to treat it as a lesser, inferior being, and beings of
related races will also find it unpleasant to be around.
The creature suffers a -20 penalty on all Social skills
other than Intimidation.
Elemental Blending ...............................................10
The creature is or appears to be composed of a par-
ticular element, and as a result, whenever it is fully
immersed in that element, it is effectively invisible.
Elemental Incohesion .......................................... -10
The material of this creature’s body is volatile and
unstable. It needs to be sustained by a certain amount
of its own elemental energy in the surroundings. If
the surroundings are extremely favorable, the being
may stay for 1-10 days, such as a fire elemental on
an active volcano or an air elemental in a hurricane.
If the surroundings aren’t favorable, but also not det-
rimental, such as an air elemental on a world with an
air atmosphere, it may remain 1-10 hours, or just 1-10
minutes if the prevailing elemental conditions are
```
unfavorable (a water elemental in the desert, a cold
```
elemental in the tropics, an earth elemental in a cloud
```
castle). Summoned creatures will automatically return
```
to their home at the end of this duration. If the creature
is not able to withdraw to an environment of its own
pure element, it will suffer the effects of an unmodified
```
Ball attack of the opposing element (Earth suffers a
```
Lightning Ball, Fire suffers a Cold Ball, Ice and Water
suffer a Fire Ball, Light and Wind suffer a Lightning
```
Ball doing unresistable Impact criticals) of its own
```
```
size, once each interval (every day, hour, or minute).
```
Flattened Body ........................................................5
The creature’s low profile allows it to gain the benefits
of concealment and its aquatic mobility in water much
shallower than would be expected for a creature of its
size. For ordinary creatures, assume they require at
least 6" of water depth for Diminutive, 1' for Small, 4'
for Medium, 8' for Large, 15' for Big, 25' for Large, 50'
for Huge, and 100' for Gigantic. Creatures with this
talent are treated as if they were four sizes smaller for
```
this purpose (e.g., a Gigantic Flattened body creature
```
```
can operate in 8' of water, or an Enormous one in 15').
```
Gills...........................................................................0
The creature is able to filter oxygen out of the water,
```
but it is unable to breathe in air. (For creatures able
```
to breath both air and water, use the Air and Water
```
Breathing talent instead.)
```
Incorporeal ............................................................50
The creature is not physically in the material world,
instead lingering in the spirit world. The creature may
choose to manifest visually and physically for short
times with its specified physical statistics. During
these times, the incorporeal being seems real and sol-
id. When not manifesting physically, this creature may
not affect the physical world in any way. When man-
ifested physically it may be hit by magical weapons,
but it cannot bleed, be staggered or be stunned, and
all criticals are reduced by one level of severity. In
addition, it has +50 DB at all times.
Incorporeal Limbs ................................................10
The creature’s limbs are semi-incorporeal, although
its body and head are fully corporeal. It takes normal
damage from attacks but criticals dealt to the limbs are
negated 25% of the time.

Creature Law
Increased Reach ........................................... 5 / Tier
Elongated limbs allow the creature to make attacks
and manipulate objects 5' per Tier beyond the normal
area of control for its size.
Induce Breakage..................................... 10+2 / Tier
Because of its solid construction, every time the being
is struck by any item capable of breaking, roll a break-
age check with a penalty of -10 per Tier.
Landing ..................................................................10
The creature lands from a fall with grace, on its feet
and with an unusual ability to withstand the impact.
Any falling distance is reduced by 5' per level.
Lifeless....................................................................60
Creatures which are animated by magic rather than
life are immune to life draining, constitution draining,
poisons and diseases, and spells that affect life force
or cause death.
Prehensile Appendage ...........................................10
The creature has a tail, trunk, or other appendage
which is dexterous enough to hold and grasp objects.
Projected Form......................................................50
The creature’s true location is somewhere else, or it is
incorporeal and projects a body on the physical realm,
which is real in all aspects. If killed or destroyed it
will vanish. The creature cannot project a new body
for 24 hours.
Protected Senses and Breathing...........................15
The creature’s airways and other vulnerable openings
can be closed to protect the creature from harmful dust
and sand. The creature can withstand even a sand-
storm.
Shapechange, Face ................................................10
By shifting its face, the creature may take on the ap-
```
pearance of any humanoid race (although it may need
```
```
to cover its body to make the disguise convincing).
```
It can even take an appearance identical to a specific
person it has had time to observe closely.
Shapechange, Flawed.......................................... -25
Although the creature may have talents or spells
which allow it to change its form, one feature or part
of its body will always remain unchanged, revealing
its nature to those who spot it.
Shapechange, Phase ..............................................30
The creature transforms itself gradually to several
distinct phases, every 8 hours. Each of its forms has
a different appearance, different powers and even a
different profession or archetype.
Shapechange, Single Form ...................................40
The creature is able to shapechange between two dis-
```
tinct forms (e.g., between an animal and a human). It
```
may also take intermediate forms, but cannot take the
form of other creatures or change distinctive features.
For example, if it takes a human form, it will always
be recognizable as the same human form, not different
individuals.
Shapechange, Therianthropy ...............................30
The therianthrope is able to transform into a particular
animal form or a half-animal intermediate form. How-
ever, this is due to a curse or transmissible disease and
in order to control the transformation and the animal
form, they will need to use Control Lycanthropy skill.
Shapechange, True ................................................50
Creature may change form as it wishes. Taking specif-
ic forms require some amount of study. Changing to a
specific being allows for +20 disguise.
Shapechange, Type ................................................20
Able to take on any form within a particular type, the
creature has very fine control of their form and is even
able to take the form of specific individuals whose
```
appearance it is familiar with (gaining +20 on any
```
```
Disguise maneuver). However, the creature is unable
```
to take the form of a different type or category of crea-
ture. For example, a humanoid can take any humanoid
form, but not the form of an animal.
Size Change............................................................40
```
The creature is able to increase or decrease its Size (by
```
```
one Size category) at will.
```
Size Change, Limited ............................................15
```
The creature is able to increase or decrease its Size (by
```
```
one Size category) for up to 5 minutes once per hour.
```
Traction ....................................................................7
The creature can maintain good footing even on dif-
ficult surfaces such as sand and loose rock. It suffers
no maneuver penalty or reduction in speed on such
surfaces. Penalties for more difficult surfaces, such as
mud, ice, and oil, are halved.
Twisted Form ....................................................... -25
Hideous and corrupted, the creature is immediately
recognized as a force of evil and will be shunned or
attacked. Depending on the circumstances, this may
be treated as a -100 reaction modifier, or a fear RR
may be appropriate. Anyone interacting in a peaceful
fashion with the creature will likewise be treated as an
evil threat.
Unbreathing ...........................................................20
The creature does not need to breathe. It cannot be
drowned or suffocate, and is not affected by inhaling
any type of gas or liquid.
Wings, Folding.......................................................10
```
The creature’s wings (from the Wings, Flying talent)
```
fold away or disappear beneath a protective carapace,
so that they will not be damaged by attacks unless
```
they are specifically targeted (as a called shot) or if the
```
creature is in flight.

Creature Law
SENSES
Any creature needs at least one type of sense to be
able to survive. Normally all creatures have a number
of senses to more fully be aware of their surroundings
and Increase their survival. The basic senses sight,
hearing, smell, taste and touch are assumed to be
present, as well as the less well known senses of bal-
ance, temperature and pressure. These senses might be
missing, or enhanced from their normal human base-
line and will be listed under the senses talents.
Poor senses will be listed as a penalty and acute nor-
mal senses will impart a bonus to the Perception skill,
but some senses will be given qualifications of animal
senses. These impart a bonus and give more and im-
proved abilities in their sensory range and power.
Air Movement Detection ......................................25
Using tiny sensory hairs or other mechanisms, the
creature is able to sense fine air movements which
allow it to form an accurate picture of its immediate
surroundings within a 1'/Tier radius.
All-Around Vision .................................................30
With a plethora of eyes or other means of detection,
the creature sees in all directions and will never be
surprised by a visible foe, and attacks against it are
never treated as flank or rear attacks.
Electrolocation, Active ..........................................20
By discharging electrical impulses, the creature may
sense its surroundings and detect any creatures near it,
within 1'/level. Electrolocation is normally only prac-
tical in water, especially salt water, although magical
creatures may be able to overcome this limitation. Ac-
tive electrolocation can distinguish different materials
and is particularly sensitive to living creatures.
Electrolocation, Passive ........................................15
The creature is sensitive to subtle electrical fields, in-
cluding those generated by living creatures. The crea-
ture may detect any living creature or other electrical
source within 1'/level.
Hearing, Echolocation ..........................................30
Production of carefully tuned calls and precise hearing
allow the creature to form an image of its surround-
ings and movements of creatures in the area. It is able
to hear ultrasound and gains a +40 bonus to hear-
```
ing-based Perception maneuvers (+20 for Perception
```
```
that includes hearing as well as other senses). For most
```
purposes, the creature should be treated as “seeing”
everything within 100'.
Hearing, Elephant .................................................25
The creature’s hearing is equivalent to that of an ele-
phant. Elephants have good hearing for both high and
```
low sounds, and can detect extremely low sounds (in-
```
```
cluding infrasound) from sources up to 7 miles away.
```
They have a +30 bonus to hearing-based Perception
```
maneuvers (+15 for Perception that includes hearing
```
```
as well as other senses).
```
Invisibility Sense....................................................25
The creature is able to clearly see invisible creatures
and objects within 5' per level, as if they were not in-
visible.
Life Sense .............................................. 10 + 5 / Tier
The creature senses all living things within a 5' per
Tier radius, and is able to distinguish between strong
and weak life essence.
Presence Sense .......................................................10
Minds of sentient creatures are easily detectable to this
creature within a 5' per level radius. Treat as the Men-
```
talism spell Presence (on the Detections open list).
```
Sight, Demon .........................................................70
The creature’s sight is not bound by normal lim-
```
itations. It can see in complete darkness (including
```
```
magical darkness) up to 100' and Darkness penalties
```
beyond that distance are reduced by 40. It can also see
invisible objects and creatures within 5' per level, and
```
has Thermal Vision (see the talent below).
```
Sight, Far-sighted .................................................. -5
The creature can see far off objects clearly, but nearby
objects are out of focus. Searching and close-up in-
spection suffer a -20 penalty.
Sight, Gecko ...........................................................10
These beings have good eyes. They see color at night,
can focus on objects at multiple distances at once, and
see much more detail up close.
Sight, Near-sighted ................................................ -5
The creature can see nearby objects clearly, but far-
away objects are out of focus. Vision beyond 10' suf-
fers a -20 penalty.
Sight, Stomatopod .................................................25
The creature has the visual capabilities of a stomato-
```
pod. Stomatopods (or mantis shrimp) have compound
```
eyes capable of seeing 12 different colors, including
into the ultraviolet range, can distinguish polarized
light, and do not lose color vision in dark conditions.
Any attempt to spot a camouflaged or invisible ob-
ject gains a +50 bonus, and other visual Perception
```
maneuvers gain a +20 bonus (+10 for Perception that
```
```
includes vision as well as other Senses).
```
Sight, Wide Angle ..................................................20
The creature has wide set or additional eyes providing
a view to the flanks. Attackers gain no positional bo-
nus for Flank and the creature gains a +20 bonus on
Reverse Strike skill.

Creature Law
Thermal Vision ......................................................25
The creature is able to see temperature differences,
permitting it to spot warm and cold objects even in
complete darkness, up to 50' away. Invisibility does
not mask temperature differences, so within this range
it can distinguish objects that are warmer or colder
```
than the background (such as people). Thermal vision
```
```
provides limited detail (-20 Perception for fine dis-
```
```
crimination if it is the creature’s only form of vision).
```
Tremorsense ...........................................................10
The creature Senses vibrations with enough precision
to locate creatures moving within 50' and determine
their approximate size. The creature is also able to
```
hear infrasound (sounds too low for humans to hear).
```
Water Current Detection ......................................20
Using a lateral line or other means, the creature is able
to sense fine water currents which allow it to form an
accurate picture of its immediate surroundings within
a 20' radius.
Rmcl 2.6. skills & talents
With profession, race, culture, and stats in hand,
it is now time to make the core of the character:
```
skills. Development Points (DP) are spent each
```
level to develop and train ranks in skills. Training
```
and natural aptitude (based on stats) affect one’s
```
chance of accomplishing certain actions and ac-
```
tivities (e.g., fighting, maneuvering, spellcasting,
```
```
etc.). The complete list of skills and descriptions
```
is in Chapter 3. Costs for skills are determined by
```
one’s profession (Section 2.4).
```
Characters start with 60 DP each level plus a
number of bonus DP based on race. The bonus
DP may be spent on talents at character creation
or spent during early levels instead. Up to 25 bo-
nus DP are spent each level until they are all spent
```
(e.g., a human who spends nothing on talents gets
```
```
60 + 25 = 85 DP for each of Level 1 and 2).
```
The GM may also allow characters to spend DP
on talents. While some races automatically receive
special talents, other abilities, whether cultural
or individual, may be purchased as talents and
flaws. While skills are trained in small increments
```
over time, talents and flaws rather represent (dis)
```
abilities or conditions that one either has or does
not. While skills can be taken many times over to
improve them, talents are taken once or a limited
number of times, depending on the talent. Descrip-
tions for the talents and flaws, along with their DP
```
costs (which are the same for all Professions), are
```
in Chapter 4.
```
Example: Takko spent much of his childhood
```
helping his grandfather prepare medicines and
poisons, so he was exposed to non-lethal doses
```
of many toxins over a long period of time; this
```
has trained his body to be more resistant to poi-
sons than is normal. The player and GM agree
that Takko should purchase the Physical Re-
```
sistance talent at Tier II (+10 to RRs vs. Phys-
```
```
ical Resistance Rolls) for 10 DP, even though
```
normally this is a racial talent.
```
Example: Volosha who begins as a Fighter of the
```
Langur may be able to ride a horse extremely
well because her nomadic people are taught to
ride at an extremely early age even for Nomads.
The GM decides that the character may begin
the game not only with the cultural ranks a
```
nomad receives, but also the Prodigy (Riding)
```
```
II talent (for 12 DP), which gives her +10 to
```
Riding.

UNIFIED

Creature LawChapter 5:Chapter 5:
Animals, MundaneAnimals, Mundane
50
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
5. Anim als5. Anim als
These Creatures are defined as being natural to the
world. Their form is based on adapting for survival
and they are the best nature can provide. They have
skills and powers dealing with survival and subsis-
tence. Natural camouflage, quadrupedal Movement,
or better, and modest body weaponry are among their
benefits. On the downside they are not so bright and
rely on trial and error and instinct to make their way
in the world. They are typically the prey for preda-
tors, although a small portion have become predators.
They are sometimes domesticated for their qualities
as beasts of burden, or as little helpers on other fronts.
They can be trained and manipulated as their masters
see fit. They serve as food, or to hunt food. Animals
have enhanced Senses normally and a good portion
have adapted to a nocturnal life, or underground. Their
well-developed Senses let them escape from preda-
tion. One more disadvantage is that animals cannot
figure out how to use tools and gear. Only the bright-
est, shrewdest animals use sticks and stones to their
advantage.
Most animals have one or more Movement talents, the
No Equipment and Animalistic flaws, some form of
Natural Weaponry, and Acute senses or other sensory
talents.
5.1. aeRial animals
Aerial animals reign freely in the sky. They mostly
lair on land, but their hunting grounds are among the
clouds and between mountain peaks. All use wings to
defy gravity, because it is the only non-magical way to
raise one’s head in the clouds.
BATS
Of the order Chiroptera, bats are successful predators
for many reasons. First, they are able to fly just as
well as birds. Their large, membrane-like wings serve
double duty as nets for scooping tasty insects out of
the air, with most breeds of bats then flipping their un-
dersides up to swat insects and then eating the stunned
```
insects off of their own stomachs (this occurs too fast
```
```
for the human eye to perceive). Nearly all bats possess
```
a unique echolocation tracking talent that allows them
to hunt at night as well as fly very efficiently in the
Darkness. High-pitched, ultrasonic sounds are thrown
out in front of the bat and bounced off obstructions and
flying objects. Their large ears pick up the returning
echoes, and they are analyzed as to size, direction,
Bat, minisculeBat, miniscule

```

```

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
and speed. They also receive a +15 to hearing based
Perception checks. Although most bats feed on in-
sects, some consume small animals, lizards, fish and
fruit. Three species of bat feed on animal or human
blood—thus the association with vampires. Vampire
bats consume over 40 pints of blood per year, but the
amount ingested at any one feeding is never enough to
seriously harm the victim. The major threat from this
type of bat is the transmission of disease, primarily
rabies. Bats often work together in a flock to attack
and confuse larger prey. Flocks of 50 miniscule bats
```
make a single attack at +20 OB and +1 Size; +2 Siz-
```
```
es for 100, or +3 Sizes for 200 (this is the maximum
```
```
bonus). Diminutive bats attack as a flock for +15 OB
```
and +1 Size for 10 bats, +2 Sizes for 20 bats, and +3
for 40. When in groups of 50 or more, they also cause
a confusion effect resulting in a -25 penalty to all ac-
tions of anyone engaged in combat with them in the
area of effect.
  
LIZARD, FLYING
```
Flying Lizards (also called “Flying Dragons”) glide
```
between trees with the aid of two wing-like skin flaps
along the sides of their bodies. Normally these are
kept folded. Using them, the lizard can glide for sev-
eral meters. Flying Lizards only come out of the trees
to bury their 1-4 eggs in the soil. They can transmit
diseases by touch and bite. A group of at least 5 flying
lizards may make a single attack with a +10 OB and
+1 Size, increasing to +2 Sizes for 10 or +3 Sizes for
```
20 (this is the maximum bonus). They have very good
```
sight and smell senses, resulting in being able to see
color at night and excellent vision up close resulting
in a +10 to sight -based Perception checks fairly close
by and also receive a +15 to smell based Perception
checks. Their coloration also allows them to blend
into the forest background resulting in a +25 bonus to
Stalking or a -25 penalty to Perception checks to spot
them.

52
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
5.2. avians
Birds are a specially adapted animal group. Light
bones and aerodynamic shapes allow most of them to
fly on feathered wings. Feathers provide an ideal, light
weight body covering, that aids in attaining flight.
They also protect versus the elements. Birds come in
many shapes, sizes and colors. Males tend to be more
ostentatious, to attract females. Many birds, due to
their ability to sense the Movement of subtle air flows,
can sometimes even detect invisible creatures and ob-
jects.
BIRDS, COMMON
During the course of evolution, birds have taken the
heavy boned elongated reptilian body and transformed
it into a light and efficient flying machine. Birds can be
found in all sizes, ranging from those weighing but a
```
few grams to those taller than a human (e.g., ostrich). A
```
well-honed respiratory system enables them to extract
the correct quantity of oxygen necessary for constant
wing exertion, lightning speed, and flight at altitudes
where the oxygen is thin. Plumage of both sexes is
```
usually dissimilar; with either males or females sport-
```
ing colorations to attract a mate. Males of the species
are most often slightly larger in size.
Most birds lay their eggs in a nest, where many of
their kind remain for a period of feeding and care after
birth. Birds vary dramatically by species regarding egg
laying habits, with “Indeterminant” layers laying eggs
```
roughly daily to reach their “ideal clutch” size; while
```
“Determinant” layers only laying a roughly consistent
```
number at roughly the same season (usually annually).
```
A few species can walk immediately after hatching.
The species feed on almost every type of small ani-
mal or plant matter, just one of the indications of their
amazing diversity. Many types of birds often work
together in a flock to attack larger prey. Their attacks
being increased by +1 size if there are enough of them.
Miniscule birds at least 50 in number may make a sin-
gle +20 OB attack at +1 Size, or +2 Sizes for 100 or +2
for 200. Diminutive birds at least 10 in number make
one attack at +15 OB and +1 Size, +2 Sizes for 20, or
+3 Sizes for 40.
Bird, minisculeBird, miniscule
```
Category: Animal-Avian
```

```
Archetype: Defensive
```

53
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
BIRDS OF PREY
Also known as raptors, birds of prey are birds that hunt.
Although this group technically includes owls and car-
rion birds, we are treating those groups separately due
to their distinct behaviors and traits. Here, we refer to
```
diurnal (active during daylight) birds that primarily hunt
```
rather than feeding on carrion. They include eagles, fal-
cons, hawks, and ospreys.
Raptors are powerful flyers, capable of making Swoop-
ing attacks at high speed. They are aided by keen eye-
sight permitting them to identify prey from great distanc-
es, and sharp and powerful talons to capture that prey.
They also have curved or hooked bills suited for their
carnivorous diet.
Raptors are also attentive parents. One parent will hunt
while the other attends chicks in the nest. In some spe-
cies, offspring will establish their own territories near
that of their parents.
Birds classified as Birds of Prey are often avid and
skilled hunters that often utilize diving/swooping attacks
to surprise and kill their prey quickly. As such, birds
that fall into this category are often very fast with sharp
eye sight. Sharp talons and strong legs for grasping and
killing their targets, combined with powerful and curved
beaks for tearing off flesh make for dangerous attacks.
These include Eagles, Falcons, Hawks, Harriers, Kites,
Osprey and Owls.
Eagles are formidable predators, soaring for long
periods before Swooping in to capture their prey in
their powerful talons. Eagles nest in high rocky cliffs
or extremely tall trees. Eagles can make Swooping
```
attacks (Tier II, single attack from a high altitude) to
```
even greater effect than most other birds and flying
creatures.
Birds of Prey, EagleBirds of Prey, Eagle

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
Falcons are known for their incredible swiftness and
their ability to catch prey in the air. They are the fastest
of all birds and can dive at great speeds, beyond even
that of Eagles and Hawks by way of using Swooping
```
attacks (Tier III, single attack from an extremely high
```
```
altitude). Their eye sight also allows them to spot and
```
perform their long-distance Swooping attack from
amazing heights.
These majestic birds are best characterized by their
curved beak, rounded wingtips and aggressive hunting
behavior. Hawks can be trained to hunt for humans.
Hawks are known for their incredible swiftness and
their ability to catch prey in the air. Hawks, when div-
ing long distances can attack with their first claw attack
as if it is +2 sizes due to the momentum built up in the
dive, followed by a Grapple attack the same round of
```
Diminutive size that if 100% successful (grapple crit-
```
```
ical yielding 100%) allows them to lift back off with
```
whatever they grasp up to 1/4 their weight. Hawks
usually nest in high rocky cliffs or extremely tall trees.
Hawks are formidable predators, soaring for long
periods before swooping in to attack their prey with
their powerful talons. They can also make Swooping
```
attacks (Tier II, single attack from a high altitude) to
```
even greater effect than most other birds and flying
creatures.
Hawks include 216 species and can be found in most
regions of the planet.
OWLS
Owls can be found in most areas of the world. They are
best recognized by their large head and enormous eyes
set in a circular facial disk. Owls hunt at night, feed-
ing on insects, birds and mammals the size of rabbits
or smaller. They have excellent hearing and eyesight
and soundless flight allowing them an additional +25
```
to their stalking skill (already included in their skill
```
```
value). While owls tend to attack in a fashion similar
```
to the Swooping birds shown above, they only possess
the first tier of this talent, as they usually focus on
catching relatively small prey with their talons. Thus
they do not usually hunt creatures that approach their
own size category, but instead rely on their ability to
fly nearly silently to surprise their prey.
Birds of Prey, HawkBirds of Prey, Hawk

55
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
VULTURES AND CARRION BIRDS
Birds whose diet is primarily from dead animals that
were not hunted and killed by the bird itself.
Condors are one of the largest birds in the world. They
inhabit the lonely crags of high mountains. They can
soar to great heights and often glide as far as 10 miles
without wing movements. Never attacking live ani-
mals, the condor feeds exclusively on carrion. Only
one egg is laid at a time, the young bird remains under
the care of its parents for over a year.
Owl, tinyOwl, tiny

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
Vultures can be found over an extensive portion of
the earth, feeding on carrion. They, like condors, are
solitary birds, easily recognized by their unfeathered
heads. This feature allows them to thrust their head
```
into slimy carcasses (while feeding) without ruining
```
the plumage. Their carrion feeding also results in
them often times being able to spread diseases that
they are otherwise immune to themselves.
Carrion Birds, VultureCarrion Birds, Vulture

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
5.3. caRnivoRes
Carnivorous mammals are flesh-eaters of the order
Carnivora. Generally, they are tough, or quick or
strong, to defeat their prey. Some travel in packs to
increase their effectiveness. All these creatures can
be dangerous to confront and it is a wise idea to give
them a wide berth.
```
BEARS (URSIDAE)
```
Bears are heavily-built animals with big heads and
small tails. Bears vary in size from 3' to over 8' long.
Bears are descended from dog-like ancestors.
Like dogs, bears supplement their meat diet with a
wide variety of plant matter, but also consume insects,
fruits and nuts. Bears’ short legs are deceptive for they
are able to achieve rapid gaits, up to 30 mph. During
```
the cold months, bears go into winter sleep, or torpor;
```
a state similar to hibernation. These fierce predators
have a number of natural weapons, including large
tooth-filled mouths, long claws, and powerful crush-
```
ing arms (this additional Crush critical is not subject
```
to the Optional +1 size suggestion, due to it being an
additional critical caused by a Talent and affects crea-
```
tures up to and including their own size category). To
```
find any food source, they have an incredibly powerful
sense of smell, and can track based on smell with no
Pace penalties. Bears typical form of attack is to rush
their prey, ramming them to knock them to the ground
and then trying to grapple or claw them. With a suc-
cessful grapple resulting in bite or crushing attacks.
Bear, mediumBear, medium

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
tackle larger prey, these carnivores pose little or no
threat to Medium sized beings, although some might
believe so.
Wild species of cats are generally larger and more
powerful than housecats, sharing their agility and flex-
ibility, making them effective hunters. Most stalk their
prey, often catching it by surprise, although chasing
down most prey is within their capabilities. They tend
to hunt alone.
```
CATS (FELIDAE)
```
The cat body type is powerful and very flexible. Small
cats are 19"-36" long, while Medium cats are 4'-9.5'
long. Cats belong to the family Felidae, of which there
are approximately 35 species. Cats are found world-
wide and are particularly admired for their very effi-
cient killing. They normally have 1-4 young. Cats stalk
and run down their prey, delivering death with a bite
from their strong jaws. They are often very fast and
can jump greater distances than many other animals
of similar sizes and often have a talent for Landing
without injury despite drops from heights that would
cause injury to many other animals of similar sizes.
They often have very good hearing and some can even
see well in the dark. Cats will usually start their attack
with a ram attack to try to bring their foe down, espe-
cially if they can manage to charge their foe for added
```
effect, followed by claw and bite attacks (often trying
```
```
for an artery).
```
Small and Medium Cats
House cat represents domesticated cats 14-20" long,
weighing between 6-12 lbs., although house cats
thrive when released into the wild, after which they
revert to a feral state where most can fend for them-
selves. Innate talent as hunters and abundant prey in
most biomes ensures their survival. Coloration favors
greys, yellows and blacks in a variety of patterns suit-
able for camouflage. Their prowess is wasted, how-
ever, by their tiny stature. Unable to band together to
Cat, smallCat, small

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
These cats are twice the size of a housecat, they posses
a short tail, and weigh between 11 and 35 lbs. Living in
a most strange habitat for cats: wetlands, they posses
some adaptations, such as webbed feet and nails that
cannot be completely sheathed. They enjoy swimming
and diving and can swim long distances during their
nightly forays. Males have similar calls as housecats,
but also a distinct chuckling. Unsurprisingly, they feed
mainly on fish, but will devour birds, insects, mol-
luscs, a variety of reptiles and even carrion they
happen upon.
Large Cats
Cheetahs are the fastest animals on four legs, being
able to move at speeds over 65 mph. The sleek body
```
sits atop long muscular legs; a lengthy tail helps it
```
maintain balance during a chase. The cheetah picks
out its prey, then stalks it and overtakes it with a quick
dash. The victim is usually dispatched with a bite to
the throat.
```
lions:
```
Lions are characterized by their broad
heads, large mane, and strong thick legs.
They range in size from 4.5'-6.5' long.
Usually thought of as noble or regal, these
beasts are favored by royalty and are often
used in gladiatorial-like exhibitions. Surpris-
ingly, most of a lion’s time is spent resting.
They live in groups, called prides, that are
commonly made up of 3 adult males and as
many as 15 females and young. The lionesses
do most of the hunting, preying on antelopes, ze-
bras and gazelles. Their hunting pattern consists of
a period of stalking, followed by a quick chase and
pounce. They kill, which occurs as a claw followed
by a biting grapple attack which then leads to a bite
targeting the neck or some other critically devastat-
ing portion of their prey’s body.
CheetahCheetah

ROLEMASTER


Creature Law
```
tigeRs:
```
Tigers are the biggest cats, possessing very big pow-
erful bodies and muscular legs. They are 6'-9.5' long.
Older tigers will sometimes become “man-eaters”
since man is an easier prey than the quick deer or an-
telope. Tigers are usually nocturnal creatures, living
and hunting alone. Tigers are good climbers, and are
well known for their ability to maintain a fast pace
while on the chase. Cattle, wild pigs, and deer all fall
prey to this successful hunter. Tigers stalk and pounce
upon their prey, using their great size and weight to
their advantage.
Lion, femaleLion, female

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
```
CRYPTIDS (FOLKLORE)
```
Cryptids are animals from stories and folklore. These
creatures may have existed at sometime in the past.
Without actual specimens to examine they cannot be
categorized within any animal family.
A feline head graces the gulo’s medium, fox-like body
covered with thick, white fur patterned by black mark-
ings. An unearthly beast of the northlands, they are
sought for their warm and beautiful pelts, although
only a handful of hunters and trappers dare to face
this creature alone. The thick pelt of the gulo protects
it from chill during the fiercest of blizzards, while an
insatiable appetite provides the energy required by
frigid climes. It has acquired a reputation for gluttony,
although it eats for survival rather than pleasure. Its
mournful cry can be heard at night, chilling the al-
ready cold air. Sharp oversized claws and teeth make
up this creature’s arsenal, which it uses with deadly
skill to bring down creatures much larger than itself.
Its muscular and Dense body help it to reduce damage
inflicted against it in combat, while it is also able to
immediately take advantage of any slip or mistake
that an opponent makes. It has an unusual and musky
odor due to natural oils in its fur, while it has a very
acute sense of smell and hearing that allows it to de-
tect threats. Their usual method of attack is to start
with claw attacks, but then to grapple as quickly as
possible, followed by bite attacks once the opponents
is even partially grappled.
GuloGulo

A flat, rug-like beast that flaunts its luxurious,
mink-like pelt, the rumtifusel drapes itself
over stumps while mimicking the contours
of an expensive fur. As no ancient
scripts mention this creature,
it can be assumed that it is a
relatively new and unearthly
mutation— perhaps the inadver-
tent result of a stray spell or
failed ritual within the last few
hundred years. This strange
creature feeds on those creatures
investigating its bizarre form by ly-
ing motionless until a victim is within
striking distance. It eats any manner
of flesh, sometimes saving part of the
prey for its 3-5 young that wait in a
hidden, nearby lair. When a wayfarer

64
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
Wolves are easily recognized by their powerful canine
body and thick, bushy tail. They vary in coloring from
white to yellowish brown and almost black. They
range in size from 3' to 5' long. These canine hunters
can sometimes be domesticated. Many wild and bar-
baric societies worship and revere them.
Wolves live and hunt in packs, a fact that allows them
to take on large prey such as deer, horses and caribou.
They also feed on smaller animals such as rabbits,
and mice. Wolves rely on their savage bite in combat.
They are excellent trackers due to their acute nose, but
also have very sensitive hearing.
WolfWolf

```
DOGS (CANINE)
```
Dogs are easily recognized by the generally long
muzzle and large triangular ears, usually erect. Small
dogs are 8"-15" long, while Medium dogs range up
to 5' in length. The first animal domesticated by man,
many breeds of dog have lived and worked alongside
humankind since the dawn of time. They have a very
```
keen sense of smell (similar to that of their distant
```
```
relatives bears), granting them an additional +25 to
```
smell based Perception checks, and are often used for
tracking by scent. Most dogs will start their attack by
trying to ram their foe to bring them down, at which
point they can use bite attacks or in some cases claw
attacks more effectively. Although some breeds will
start by trying to bite the neck or legs of their prey to
bring them down.
The dog family includes coyotes, wolves, foxes, and
dingos. Litters are usually comprised of 1-5 pups.
Dogs are effective hunters, using their quick speed
and endurance to wear down their prey. Hyenas are
```
not canids (they are more closely related to felines),
```
but their build and hunting style is similar enough that
```
medium dog stats can be used (although striped and
```
brown hyenas, which are largely nocturnal, also have
```
Nightvision).
```
Dog, smallDog, small

ROLEMASTER


Creature Law
MUSTELIDS/CIVETS
Mustelids include badgers, ermines, ferrets, otters, sa-
```
bles, skunk, weasels, and wolverines (detailed below),
```
among others. They range from Tiny to Small in size.
In all 67 different species the body pattern remains the
```
same: long flexible bodies connected by long necks
```
to rather small heads, short legs ending in powerful
feet, and long tails. Because of these characteristics,
they prove to be excellent swimmers, climbers, and
burrowers. Most mustelids eat primarily other small
mammals, as well as fruits, nuts, and insects.
There are 72 species of civet, of which, the mongoose
```
(detailed in section Miscellaneous Animals) is perhaps
```
the most familiar. Civets range from Tiny to Small in
size, with long bodies, elongated heads, and short legs.
They possess very keen Senses, which help in hunting.
Territory is marked with a strong secretion from their
scent glands. Civets feast on mammals, as well as in-
sects, reptiles, frogs, nuts, and berries.
The pelts of many mustelids and civets are highly
prized, and the treasure entry in the stat block reflects
the value of the pelt. A Medium Survival maneuver is
required to prepare a pelt, if it was not too damaged in
an encounter.
A common and often domesticated member of the
mustelid family of animals. They usually have brown,
black, white or mixed color fur, about 20" long includ-
ing a 5" tail. Usually ranging from 1.5 to 4.5 pounds,
with the males usually being noticeably larger. They
are quick and agile animals, usually with a social and
good natured attitude. They have advanced hearing
and sense of smell and are fairly easily trained. They
like to hunt and eat rodents and rabbits as their prima-
ry prey.
Mustelid / Civet, tinyMustelid / Civet, tiny

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
These water loving carnivores of the mustelid fami-
ly are incredibly adaptable. They make living in the
water seem luxurious as their hunting skills are honed
to perfection. As a result, they have lots of play time,
which they use to strengthen the family bond. They
weigh 11 to 70 pounds and grow between 2'6" – 5'6"
long from nose to tail. Their magnificent pelts are cov-
eted and they are hunted for it. This pelt protects them
from hypothermia by trapping a layer of air. The sea
otter spends most of its time in the water, unlike the
other varieties, blowing into their fur to maintain the
air layer. The sea otter spends most of its time in the
water, unlike the other varieties.
Skunks are the most familiar of the mustelids, being
well known for their strong gland secretions. Most
of their day is spent in the burrow or den. At night,
skunks feed on carrion, mice, insects and eggs. Their
powerful odor is an effective weapon against enemies,
causing the victim to momentarily stop breathing. If
```
they feel really threatened; they spray an area about
```
them of 2’R, covering everything near them.
Wolverines have solid bodies and possess great
strength, both of which allow them to bring down an-
imals much larger than themselves. They have keen
Senses and are good climbers. Their meat-eating is
supplemented with berries and nuts. Wolverines are
considered the most ferocious medium-sized animal.
Their feeding habits, which make it appear as if the
wolverine is voraciously devouring its meals with
growls and displays of ferocity, earned it its nicknames
of skunk bear, mountain devil, or evil spirit. They fear
no beast and have a tenacious strength and agility, un-
befitting their small stature, sometimes driving wolves
and bears from their kills.
SkunkSkunk

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
SEALS/SEA LIONS/WALRUS
Seals, Sea Lions, and Walruses are aquatic mammals
called pinnipeds. These carnivorous creatures have adapt-
ed to life in and around the sea by developing flippers
in lieu of arms and legs. Superb swimmers and divers,
they feed upon fish, mollusks, and crustaceans and spend
relatively little time on land. Their terrestrial activities
are generally confined to mating and relaxation. While
ashore, these beasts gather at “rookeries” in groups of
10-1,000. There they court, and there the males fight to
```
establish dominance. Nearly all (95%) pinnipeds prefer
```
life in cool or cold seas and have bulky, but streamlined
bodies. The majority of their weight rests in layers of in-
sulating fat. Because of their size and lack of land-orient-
ed appendages, they are cumbersome when out of water.
Both sea lions and walruses tuck their rear flippers for-
ward and waddle about. The more aquatic seals cannot
tuck their flippers and are forced to pull themselves along
on their bellies. When in water however, these creatures
are swift and graceful. Size and weight of these creatures
```
varies considerably, ranging from 100 pounds (Medium)
```
```
to several thousand pounds (lower end of Huge size).
```
Seals can reach speeds of 16 mph, while sea lions can
dive to depths of 600+ feet. Aside from their fixed hind
flippers, seals differ from walruses and sea lions by
virtue of their internal ears and an exceptionally stream-
lined body. They are the fastest pinnipeds and are ca-
```
pable of submerging for prolonged periods (as long as
```
```
15 minutes). By controlling the direct flow of blood to
```
areas other than their heart and brain, they can cut their
pulse from 120 to 4 beats per minute without fear of lost
pressure or suffocation.
Walruses are the largest pinniped, and are known for
their huge tusks and preference for icy waters and shores.
Sea lions are their close relatives. Walruses are the most
adaptable and aggressive of the pinnipeds, and have the
best sense of hearing. However, it should be noted that
their Passive and Active Parry values are for when they
are swimming, whereas on land their walking ranks and
skill value is about half, so should be divided by two
when used for defensive purposes while on land.
Sea Lion / SealSea Lion / Seal

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
MISCELLANEOUS ANIMALS
These are single representations of the Herpestidae
and Marsupial families. With only a single species,
they have been rolled into this category.
The widespread nature of the mongoose has resulted in
```
its varied appearance. Most are Tiny in size; their slender,
```
elongated bodies and small, drawn-out heads sit on top
of average to short legs. Many are excellent swimmers
```
and divers; most are expert climbers and diggers. Mon-
```
gooses feed on rodents, snakes, frogs, reptiles, scorpions,
crabs, fish and aquatic insects. They are well known for
their resistance to poison, which borders on immunity.
They routinely hunt snakes of the most poisonous variety
by offering their flanks to bite in, and then quickly swivel
to evade the strike and catch the head, biting it off.
The Tasmanian Devil is a marsupial mammal and is dis-
tantly related to the kangaroo. They resemble large ro-
dents, but have relatively huge heads and powerful jaws
which are ideal for crushing bone. Scavengers, they feed
on dead carcasses. Hungry devils may resort to predatory
ways, although this practice is not the norm. These beasts
live in dens under trees, in caves, or under rock piles.
They sleep during day and prowl at night. Their naps
often involve sunning in the open.
MongooseMongoose

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
These beasts have small skulls and wide, humped
backs which are covered with a thick bony armor
which is nearly impermeable to physical attack and
gives predators further trouble due to the protruding
spikes. Trailing out behind them are strong club-like
tails. The creatures’ range in size from 15-30' from
head to tail and weigh 3 – 4 tons. These creatures have
been known to cut wide swaths though forest vegeta-
tion, eating shrubs and such to their roots. This path
of destruction is easily identifiable by those who are
familiar with Ankylosaur habits. Ankylosaurs travel in
herds, stripping vegetation to satisfy their hunger, but
staying close together to provide the most protection
from hunters. When forced into combat by an aggres-
sor, the Ankylosaurs rely on their long, prehensile tails
which are tipped with a huge bony club. The beasts
can rotate fairly well, flicking their tails to discourage
their foes. In close combat, they can club an unfortu-
nate foe, incapacitating them with a few blows to the
legs or body.
5.4. dinosauRs
Although their heyday preceded recorded time, the
size and nature of these creatures still stirs wonder
and fear. Giant beasts which loremasters commonly
consider extinct, they are more than figures of legend.
These denizens of prehistory may yet be about, roam-
ing in the remote wild and awaiting the rebirth of their
primacy.
Dinosaurs are considered by some loremasters as
```
descended from smaller reptiles; however, this may
```
be false. Actually, while related to reptiles, they are
the distant ancestors of birds. Unlike reptiles, these
creatures are warm-blooded and quicker. Some are as
small as a hen while others reach lengths of hundreds
of feet and weigh over 100 tons. Herbivorous dino-
saurs are legion, but the most interesting examples
travel in herds or small bands and walk on all fours,
with their tails outstretched for balance. Slow to react
```
(it can take up to 2 seconds for a message to travel
```
```
from a Brontosaur’s tail to its head) and plodding, they
```
rely on their size, numbers, or body-armor to ward off
meat-eating predators. Carnivorous dinosaurs tend to
be quick, two-legged hunters that run like ostriches
and attack using their huge jaws. Always hungry and
not particular, they will stalk, ambush, or openly as-
sault almost any creature. During the hunt they rely on
their sense of smell in most cases.
Trample attacks by Dinosaurs can only occur when
```
the attacker is +2 sizes larger than the target (as noted
```
```
in Core Law 7.1).
```
Optional Rule
Trample attacks may occur when the Attacker is only
one size larger if the target has been knocked prone.
AnkylosaurAnkylosaur
```
Category: Animal-Dinosaur
```
```
Archetype: Offensive
```

72
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
Pterosaur
Some of the first creatures to evolve the ability to fly,
the pterosaurs are certainly ungainly and awkward
```
seeming; white fur covered bodies, short legs, sharply
```
curving talons, and disproportionately large wings.
The wings are actually formed of extremely thick skin
and membranes, a webbing stretched between their
```
arms and an elongated outer finger (like those of bats).
```
The wingspan of the Medium variety can reach 12'—
up to 30' for Big varieties. Primitive peoples have al-
ways given wide berth to canyons and mountain cliffs
which have Pterosaur nests sites, as these creatures
are notorious for snatching away children and live-
stock, carrying them away to their nests to feed their
young. Extremely light and awkward when walking
```
(they cannot run), they operate best in the sky. Their
```
phenomenal gliding ability enables them to soar about
for hours without flapping. They take off by stretching
their wings against the lightest of breezes. Once in air,
they manipulate the drafts and maneuver with artful
skill, searching for carrion or small beasts. Pterosaurs
have superb hearing and eyesight and, although amaz-
```
ingly light (35- 500 pounds), big individuals can sweep
```
```
down and carry off loads of up to 200 pounds (when
```
standing, however, they cannot lift off when bearing
```
any appreciable weight). Pterosaurs are most active
```
around dawn or dusk, Swooping down from their cliff
nests onto unwary prey. Ever-hungry, these beasts can
be dangerous if they take it into their heads to attack
en masse with their Big beaks, specifically evolved for
cutting and tearing food.
The apatosaur is a classic plant-eating dinosaur. Huge,
they can pick foliage out of trees thirty feet above the
ground. They possess 15'-30' bodies, excluding the
```
12'-20' neck and 15'-25' tail; they often mass as much
```
```
as 33 - 38 tons. apatosaurs move in herds of 10-100;
```
they are grazing animals and have habits similar to
those of a giraffe. Although they occasionally wade
into marshes for food or protection, they sink in mud
and do not venture into lakes. Contrary to the legends
of old loremasters, apatosaurs tend to drown when
fully submerged. The herd organization and great size
is the primary form of defense of these great creatures,
although they are capable of bashing opponents with
their huge tails. It has been seen on rare occasions that
a threatened apatosaur may elect to trample its foe,
delivering an Enormous trample attack.
```
Apatosaur (Brontosaur)Apatosaur (Brontosaur)
```

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
The Stegosaur is a peaceful herbivore with a low-
slung head and tail with an arching, bulky body which
from nose to tail can reach 30' and weigh up to 7 tons.
Its hide is armored, but its main defense is the dou-
ble row of projecting, upright plates that run along its
spine. Its 10’ - 15' tail ends in an array of four to six
spikes, each spike measuring 18"-30". The care shown
by the Stegosaur in raising its young has been noted
by sages whose area of interest lies in studying the
natural world. This display of affection and aggressive
protectiveness has given rise to the old saying, “Let
Stegosaur eggs lie.” These creatures eat a wide variety
of plant foods including leafy bushes and trees, as well
as the roots of certain plants, which they can dig up
with their spiked tails. When rearing their young, both
the male and the female will guard the nest, attacking
intruders ferociously at this time. Normally passive,
the Stegosaur can be roused if attacked or if its young
are threatened. Their tail ends, called thagomizers,
make especially effective weapons, delivering both a
nasty blow and a piercing cut with one swing.
This prehistoric creature is agile and fast moving
despite weighing between 6 and 12 tons. The bulk of
```
its 18'-27' arching body is unarmored (AT 2), but it
```
more than makes up for this fact with its massively
armored head, which is covered with thick bone which
```
sweeps up over its neck like an outstretched collar (AT
```
```
6). Two great forward-facing horns protrude above its
```
smallish eyes, while a smaller horn sprouts from the
tip of its hard nose. Sometimes used as a war-steed,
these trained creatures have a 5000-pound carrying
capacity, and give their riders a 0 modification on their
riding maneuvers. Herbivorous, the Triceratops moves
in herds across the landscape, foraging where it can.
When confronted, these creatures are far more likely
to charge than to flee. This can become a very serious
matter if the entire herd charges en masse, piercing
with their massive horns, then continuing to gore
downed foes with their impressive head armament.
StegosaurStegosaur

ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
This 22' tall brutal carnivore
sports a 6' long skull with 3'-4.5'
jaws, which in turn are bursting
with 6" teeth. Including its tail,
the Tyrannosaur can reach up to
40' in length and weigh 5-7 tons.
It possesses two small arms,
which seem small and weak look-
ing, compared to the rest of this ter-
rifying creature. Held in awe and feared
by the few cultures that have come upon
these beasts, most humanoids will avoid
Tyrannosaur territory if they can. These
“terrible lizards” are at the top of the pre-
historic food chain, feasting upon all other
creatures as it comes upon them. It is also
a loner, preferring to hunt alone from others
of its kind. In all ways, these beasts can be
considered to be the ultimate carnivore, able
to triumph over any other beast it may meet.
Fast and furious, the Tyrannosaur prowls, look-
ing for sustenance. When it finds prey it attacks
with a short burst of speed. The startled and terrified
prey may flee and some may outrun the rapidly-tiring
Tyrannosaur over a few hundred yards’ distance, but at
least one will fall victim to its quick, brutal charge and
its huge, ripping teeth. If really hungry, or excited with
the taste of blood, the tyrannosaur may enter a feeding
Frenzy which makes it even more formidable.
```
Tyrannosaur (Rex)Tyrannosaur (Rex)
```

77
ROLEMASTER
UNIFIED

Creature Law
5.5. domesticated
Those animals that are useful, or provide dietary sup-
plements, are kept by more intelligent cultures as draft,
riding and food animals. Well-fed and bred to perform
their duties, they are an important part of society, mak-
ing life much easier for their keepers. One problem
amongst these animals is Disease susceptibility and a
tendency to go along with any type of being they are
accustomed to.
Trample attacks may be possible by some domesticat-
ed animals, especially those that are hooved, but can
only occur when the attacker is +2 sizes larger than the
```
target (as noted in Core Law 7.1).
```
Optional Rule
Trample attacks may occur when the Attacker is only
one size larger if the target has been knocked prone.
CAMELS
Camels and their relatives, alpacas and llamas, are
the domesticated members of the camel family. These
beasts are noted for their unique two-toe foot struc-
ture, with wide, padded soles adapted to travel on soft,
sandy soils. Their long necks support relatively small
heads, and their teeth are designed to cut and chew
tough, often thorny vegetation. Each has a three cham-
bered stomach that is ideal for their vegetarian diet.
The two camel species, dromedaries and bactrian
camels, both have large back humps that store fat and
provide a food and water reserve for times of famine
or drought.
Dromedaries can store and lose amounts of fat equal
to over 25% of their body weight and, in order to re-
plenish their reserves, have been known to consume
over 26 gallons of water in 5-10 minutes. Thus, both
types are ideally suited for slow,
steady travel in rugged,
remote reaches.
The one-hump, short-haired dromedaries are masters
of the hot, arid desert, while the two-humped bactrian
is less specialized and works well in cold or hot re-
gions. In winter or in cool regions, the bactrian grows
a long coat, but in warm climes it sheds its hair to be-
come virtually naked. Their great strength allows them
```
carry great loads (racing dromedary: 220 lbs. draft
```
```
dromedary: 400 lbs. and a Bactrian camel may carry
```
```
up to 700 lbs), but they are generally slow animals and
```
are temperamental, dirty, and given to spitting.
Camel, Dromedary, DraftCamel, Dromedary, Draft
```

```
Alpacas and llamas are smaller than camels and
have no humps. Both are domesticated forms of the
guanaco, beasts adapted to high altitudes and arid or
semi-arid locales. Rugged, fast, and lively, they are
good pack animals and produce fine fleece. Although
they cannot support a full-grown man like a camel
can, alpacas and llamas are superb climbers and can
bear 90 pounds of goods up seemingly invincible
grades.
ELEPHANTS
Domesticated elephants have less of a wild streak
than wild elephants. They are typically of the Indian
variety. They bond with a single mahud, who lives
with the elephant night and day and cares for it with
zeal, lest the elephant starts to dislike him. Domesti-
```
cated elephants haul goods (carrying capacity without
```
```
encumbrance: 2400 Lbs.), work as mobile cranes and
```
do other jobs too heavy for any other creature. They
uproot smaller trees, move boulders and carry men
into battle. Although a typical elephant needs about
400 pounds of fodder each day, about half of that can
be foraged by the animal on the move, if the territory
is suitable.
The largest land animals, elephants are huge her-
bivores and may weigh up to 13,000 pounds. They
are not above consuming as much as 450 pounds of
vegetation in a single day. Elephants fan themselves
with their large ears, feed themselves with a long
```
prehensile trunk (actually an elongated nose and
```
```
upper lip), and walk on thick, pillar-like legs that
```
end in flat, padded feet. They use their size, their
trunk, and a pair of curved 50-100 pound ivory tusks
when in combat. Although they rest at midday and
twice in the night, they are otherwise constantly
active. Elephants have poor eyesight, but otherwise
their senses are excellent. Young males may operate
alone or in small groups, but elephants are primarily
social beasts and prefer to travel in herds led by an
old female. All rely on leaves, shoots, grass, and fruit
for their diet, feeding and drinking with their nimble
trunks.
Indian elephants differ from the African variety by
virtue of their smaller ears, gently humped back, and
smaller stature. With work, most elephants can be
domesticated, which adds the Control submission III
```
[+15 bonus] (-15 DP) Talent to their listed Talents,
```
for someone who has the Animal, Riding- Elephant
and/or Animal Handling- Elephant skills
